## 24. DELIVERABLES

### 24.1 Capability Matrix

| Subsystem | Core Capabilities | Advanced Capabilities | Future Capabilities |
|-----------|------------------|----------------------|---------------------|
| **Orchestration** | Graph-based workflow, state management, checkpointing | Dynamic replanning, parallel execution, human-in-the-loop | Self-optimizing orchestration, adaptive routing |
| **Memory** | Working (context), Episodic (checkpoints) | Semantic (knowledge graph), Procedural (skills) | Unified memory with automatic tiering, memory-as-service |
| **Planning** | Static planning, task decomposition | Dynamic replanning, budget-aware planning | Learned planning from experience |
| **Multi-Agent** | Supervisor-worker, router-specialist | Market-based coordination, blackboard | Emergent collaboration protocols |
| **Self-Improvement** | Reflection, critique | Performance tracking, experience replay | Automatic prompt/tool optimization |
| **Evaluation** | LLM-as-judge, golden datasets | Pass^k, adversarial testing | Continuous eval in production |
| **Knowledge** | RAG (vector), structured DB | Hybrid retrieval (vector + graph + SQL) | Self-maintaining knowledge base |
| **Tool Usage** | MCP integration, typed schemas | Dynamic tool loading, error recovery | Tool learning from success/failure |
| **Guardrails** | Schema validation, allowlists | Policy engine, budget caps | Learned guardrails from interaction |
| **Observability** | Logging, tracing | Metrics, dashboards, alerts | Predictive failure detection |

### 24.2 Comparison Tables

**Architecture Comparison:**

| Architecture | Production Use | Benchmark Perf | Complexity | Cost | Reliability | Scalability |
|-------------|---------------|----------------|------------|------|-------------|-------------|
| ReAct | Medium | Low-Med | Low | Low | Low | Low |
| Plan-Execute | High | Med | Med | Med | Med-High | Med |
| Reflection | High | Med-High | Med | 2-4× | High | Low |
| ToT/GoT | None | High (puzzles) | High | 10-100× | Med | None |
| Hierarchical | Very High | High | Med-High | Med-High | High | High |
| Router | Very High | Med-High | Med | Low | High | Very High |
| Hybrid | High | Very High | High | High | High | Med-High |
| MAD | Low | Med | High | Very High | Low | Low |
| MoA | Medium | High | Med | 3-5× | High | Med |

**Memory System Comparison:**

| System | Persistence | Retrieval Latency | Scalability | Best For | Worst For |
|--------|------------|-------------------|-------------|----------|-----------|
| Context Window | Session only | Instant | Very Low | Current task state | Cross-session recall |
| Vector Store | Cross-session | 10-100ms | High | Semantic similarity | Exact matches, relationships |
| Knowledge Graph | Cross-session | 1-50ms (indexed) | Med-High | Relationships, hierarchies | Unstructured text |
| SQL Database | Cross-session | 1-10ms (indexed) | Very High | Structured records, transactions | Fuzzy search |
| Filesystem | Cross-session | 1-100ms (IO-speed) | Very High | Large artifacts, code, documents | Search without indexing |
| Checkpoint (State) | Cross-session | 50-500ms | Medium | Full state recovery | Retrieval (not designed for it) |

**Orchestration Framework Comparison:**

| Feature | LangGraph | CrewAI | OpenAI SDK | MAF | PydanticAI |
|---------|-----------|--------|------------|-----|------------|
| Graph-based | Native | No | No | Event-driven | No |
| State schema | Required | Implicit | Minimal | Implicit | Typed |
| Checkpointing | First-class | Via memory | No | No | No |
| Human-in-loop | First-class | Callbacks | Handoff | Planned | No |
| Multi-agent | Subgraphs | Native | Handoffs | Native | Via composition |
| Memory | External | Built-in 4 types | Context only | SK memory | Context only |
| Type safety | Partial (Pydantic) | Partial | None | .NET native | First-class |
| Learning curve | Steep | Gentle | Easy | Medium | Medium |
| Debugging | LangSmith | Callbacks | Console | VS Code | Python debugger |

### 24.3 Patterns & Anti-Patterns Catalog

**Design Patterns (DO use):**

| Pattern | Problem Solved | When to Use |
|---------|---------------|-------------|
| **Supervisor+Workers** | Complex task decomposition | Any multi-domain task |
| **Router+Specialists** | Cost optimization | Different complexity levels in tasks |
| **Context Isolation** | Context overflow, memory pollution | Any multi-step agent |
| **Filesystem-as-Memory** | Infinite context for artifacts | Large-output tasks (code, research, documents) |
| **Plan-Verify-Execute** | Brittle plans | Any plan-based architecture |
| **Reflection-Then-Output** | Poor quality first drafts | Any output-generating task |
| **Tiered Model Routing** | Cost-quality balance | Mixed-complexity workflows |
| **Shadow Deployment** | Safe testing | First deployment of any agent |
| **Human-in-the-Loop Escalation** | High-stakes decisions | Financial, legal, medical, safety-critical |
| **Living Plan (todo.md)** | Attention steering | Any multi-step agent |

**Anti-Patterns (NEVER use):**

| Anti-Pattern | Why It Fails | Better Alternative |
|-------------|--------------|-------------------|
| **LLM as Guardrail** | Model bypasses its own constraints | Code-level guardrails (schemas, policies, budgets) |
| **Circular Delegation** | Infinite loop, unbounded cost | Cycle detection, max depth, explicit handoff protocol |
| **Role Overlap** | Agent conflict, inconsistent outputs | Clear role boundaries, one responsibility per agent |
| **Over-Delegation** | Coordination overhead > productive work | Consolidate simpler tasks, only delegate when necessary |
| **Whole-Context Dump** | Attention dilution, token waste | Context engineering: reduce, offload, isolate |
| **Prompt-as-Security** | Prompt injection bypasses it | Code-level auth, secret management, input sanitization |
| **Single Model for Everything** | Wrong model for wrong task | Router + specialist models per task type |
| **No Termination Condition** | $10,000 bill for nothing | Always: max steps, max time, max cost, goal check |
| **Synchronous Multi-Agent** | Sequential when parallel is better | DAG-based execution, async where possible |
| **Golden Path Only Testing** | 80% demo, breaks in production | Testing pyramid: unit → integration → E2E → adversarial → HITL |

### 24.4 Agent Specification Template

```yaml
# Agent Specification Template
# Use this to define every new agent in the ecosystem

agent:
  id: agent-researcher-v1
  name: Research Agent
  version: 1.0.0
  type: specialized  # general | specialized | utility | supervisor | worker
  
identity:
  role: Researcher
  persona: "Senior Research Analyst with expertise in multi-source synthesis"
  experience: 15  # years of domain experience to model
  expertise:
    - Information retrieval
    - Source credibility assessment
    - Multi-perspective synthesis
    - Citation management
    
mission:
  purpose: "Conduct comprehensive research across multiple sources and produce structured, cited reports"
  scope: "Web-based research, document analysis, data synthesis. NOT: code generation, financial decisions, customer interaction"
  
goals:
  primary: "Produce accurate, well-cited research reports"
  secondary:
    - "Identify conflicting viewpoints and resolve them"
    - "Flag uncertainty and information gaps"
    - "Preserve source traceability"
    
success_criteria:
  - "All claims cite verifiable sources"
  - "Multiple perspectives represented for controversial topics"
  - "Confidence scores attached to each finding"
  - "Information gaps explicitly documented"
  
constraints:
  must_not:
    - "Invent sources or citations"
    - "Make claims without supporting evidence"
    - "Share confidential information"
    - "Make financial/medical/legal recommendations"
  always:
    - "Flag low-confidence findings"
    - "Distinguish fact from opinion"
    - "Include date of information retrieval"
    
inputs:
  - name: query
    type: string
    description: "Research question or topic"
    required: true
    max_length: 2000
  - name: sources
    type: array[string]
    description: "Preferred sources (optional, else auto-selected)"
    required: false
    max_items: 10
  - name: depth
    type: enum[shallow, medium, deep]
    default: medium
    description: "Research depth vs speed tradeoff"
    
outputs:
  - name: report
    type: structured
    schema:
      summary: string
      findings: array[{
        finding: string,
        evidence: array[{source: url, quote: string, date: date}],
        confidence: float (0-1),
        caveats: array[string]
      }]
      information_gaps: array[string]
      methodology: array[string]
      
tools:
  required:
    - web_search
    - web_extract
    - source_verify
    - cite_manager
  optional:
    - pdf_reader
    - youtube_transcript
    - github_search
    - academic_search
  excluded:
    - code_execution
    - file_write (other than report output)
    - api_call (other than search tools)
    
memory:
  working: true
  episodic:
    enabled: true
    retention: "30 days"
    storage: postgres
  semantic:
    enabled: true
    via: knowledge_graph
    scope: "Sources used, credibility scores, domain concepts"
  procedural:
    enabled: true
    skills:
      - "source_credibility_assessment"
      - "multi_source_synthesis"
      - "citation_formatting"
      
reasoning:
  architecture: hybrid  # plan-and-execute + reflection
  loop: "Plan → Search → Analyze → Synthesize → Verify → Reflect → Output"
  mental_models:
    primary: scientific_method
    secondary:
      - first_principles
      - mece
      
planning:
  enabled: true
  type: dynamic
  max_steps: 20
  replan_on_failure: true
  budget_aware: true
  plan_path: "living_todo.md"
  
reflection:
  enabled: true
  frequency: post_step  # post_step | post_task | both
  depth: deep
  save_to_memory: true
  
evaluation:
  method: llm_as_judge
  metrics:
    - citation_accuracy
    - comprehensiveness
    - bias_detection
    - information_gap_coverage
  pass_threshold: 0.7  # must score 0.7+ to deliver
  
guardrails:
  policies:
    - "No source fabrication"
    - "Must distinguish fact from opinion"
    - "Must flag stale information (>1 year without re-verify)"
  enforcement: code_level  # code_level > prompt_level
  escalation:
    trigger: "confidence < 0.5 or policy violation"
    target: "human_review_queue"
    
error_recovery:
  max_retries: 3
  fallback_strategy: "alternative_source"
  failure_escalation: true
  
confidence:
  calibration: true
  method: htc  # Holistic Trajectory Calibration
  minimum_to_proceed: 0.6
  
observability:
  tracing: true
  logging: structured_json
  metrics:
    - tokens_per_task
    - steps_per_task
    - cost_per_task
    - source_accuracy
    - task_completion_time
    
cost:
  max_per_task: 5.00  # USD
  routing: tiered
  tiers:
    planning: frontier
    execution: medium
    verification: frontier
    
interfaces:
  mcp_tools: true
  agent_to_agent: a2a_protocol
  human: rest_api
  
versioning:
  prompt_version: v1.2
  tool_version: v2.0
  memory_schema_version: v1.0
  
ownership:
  team: "Research Platform Team"
  maintainer: "team-research-platform@company.com"
  review_schedule: "monthly"
```

### 24.5 Maturity Model

| Level | Name | Characteristics | Architecture | Memory | Self-Improvement | Governance | Testing |
|-------|------|---------------|-------------|--------|-----------------|------------|---------|
| **L0** | **Naive** | Single prompt, no tools, no state | Chatbot | None | None | None | Manual |
| **L1** | **Augmented** | LLM + tools, basic function calling | ReAct loop | Context only | None | API key only | Golden path |
| **L2** | **Workflow** | Structured pipeline, multiple steps | Plan-and-Execute | Context + file artifacts | Manual reflection | Input validation | Unit + Integration |
| **L3** | **Agent** | Autonomous, tool-using, stateful | Graph-based (LangGraph) | Working + Episodic checkpoints | Post-task reflection | Auth + Audit + Budget | Full pyramid |
| **L4** | **Multi-Agent** | Specialized agents, supervisor | Hierarchical (Supervisor+Workers) | All 3 (working, episodic, semantic) | Performance tracking + experience replay | RBAC + Policy engine | + Adversarial |
| **L5** | **Learning** | Self-improving, adapting | Hybrid (optimal per domain) | Full memory stack (all 4 types) | Full learning loop (bake-off) | Full governance framework | + Continuous eval |
| **L6** | **Autonomous** | Self-governing, emergent collaboration | Self-optimizing | Unified memory with auto-tiering | Auto-improvement (human exception) | Self-policing + human oversight | Self-testing |

**Transition criteria between levels:**
- L0→L1: Need to access external data/tools
- L1→L2: Tasks consistently need 3+ steps
- L2→L3: Reliability requirements emerge (pause/resume, error recovery)
- L3→L4: Need multiple domains of expertise in one task
- L4→L5: Performance plateaus, need improvement beyond prompt tweaking
- L5→L6: Business requirements demand minimal human intervention

### 24.6 Implementation Roadmap

**Phase 1: Foundation (Months 1-2)**
- Set up agent infrastructure (LangGraph + PydanticAI)
- Build Context Engineering pipeline (reduction, offloading, isolation)
- Implement basic observability (tracing, logging, metrics)
- Create agent registry and specification system
- Set up CI/CD with eval gates
- **Milestone:** 1 production agent with full harness, tested and monitored

**Phase 2: Single-Agent Excellence (Months 3-4)**
- Implement full memory stack (working → episodic → semantic)
- Add reflection and self-improvement loops
- Implement guardrails and safety policies
- Build evaluation framework (LLM-as-judge + golden dataset)
- Create agent specification template
- **Milestone:** 3 specialized agents, each achieving 85%+ task completion in production

**Phase 3: Multi-Agent Coordination (Months 5-6)**
- Implement Router + Specialists pattern
- Add Supervisor+Workers for complex tasks
- Build shared workspace and inter-agent communication
- Implement conflict resolution and escalation
- Add A2A protocol support
- **Milestone:** Multi-agent system handling cross-domain tasks, 70%+ cost reduction via routing

**Phase 4: Self-Improvement & Scale (Months 7-8)**
- Implement performance tracking and experience replay
- Add automatic prompt improvement (A/B testing)
- Build agent marketplace/registry for discovery
- Implement full governance framework
- Add shadow deployment for new agents
- **Milestone:** System running 10+ agents, <10% regression rate on updates, self-improvement cycle active

**Phase 5: Autonomous Operations (Months 9-12)**
- Implement full learning loop
- Add autonomous conflict resolution
- Build predictive failure detection
- Implement auto-scaling agent pools
- **Milestone:** System running 20+ agents, <5% human intervention rate, continuous improvement active

**Risks & Mitigations:**

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| Cost explosion | Medium | Critical | Tiered routing, budget caps, cost monitoring |
| Hallucination cascade | High | High | Verification layer at every step |
| Framework instability | Medium | High | Abstract core logic from framework |
| Memory corruption | Medium | High | Isolated memory per agent, validation |
| Agent conflicts | High | Medium | Role boundaries, escalation protocol |
| Model behavior changes | High | Medium | Shadow deployment, eval gates, pin models |
| Team learning curve | High | Medium | Start with simple agents, pair programming |
| Benchmark overfitting | Low | Medium | Diversify eval datasets, real-world testing |

### 24.7 Gap Analysis

**Solved Problems (consensus exists):**
- Basic tool-use (ReAct works)
- Graph-based orchestration (LangGraph pattern)
- Context engineering (reduction, offloading, isolation)
- MCP as tool integration standard
- Evaluation via LLM-as-judge
- Hierarchical multi-agent (Supervisor+Workers)
- Tiered model routing for cost optimization
- Checkpoint-based state persistence

**Open Problems (consensus emerging but not settled):**
- Memory architecture (how to integrate all 4 types efficiently)
- Long-term memory management (decay, pruning, conflict resolution)
- Agent-to-agent communication standards (A2A vs custom protocols)
- Automatic prompt optimization (how much to automate vs human-gate)
- Self-improvement loops (how to prevent regression)
- Multi-agent conflict resolution (deterministic vs negotiated)
- Agent security (preventing prompt injection at system level)
- Confidence calibration in production

**Unsolved Problems (active research, no consensus):**
- True generalization across tasks without retraining
- Reliable long-horizon planning (50+ steps with complex dependencies)
- Emergent multi-agent coordination without explicit programming
- Agent debugging (nondeterministic failures are hard to reproduce)
- Learning from limited experience (sample-efficient agent improvement)
- Interpretable agent decision-making (why did the agent do that?)
- Agent-to-agent trust establishment (can I trust this agent's output?)
- Automated agent creation from natural language specifications

**Emerging Opportunities:**
1. **Memory-as-a-Service** — Dedicated memory infrastructure for agents (Mem0, Zep, custom)
2. **Agent Harness Frameworks** — Focus on the harness, not the agent loop (current gap)
3. **Agent Observability Platforms** — Specialized tracing for agent behavior (beyond LLM tracing)
4. **Cross-Platform Agent Interoperability** — Agents from different ecosystems collaborating (A2A + MCP)
5. **Domain-Specific Agent Benchmarks** — General benchmarks don't capture domain expertise
6. **Agent Development Environments** — IDEs designed for agent building, testing, debugging
7. **Agent Version Control** — Git for agents (prompts, tools, evals, templates as code)
8. **Automated Agent Testing** — Fuzzing, adversarial testing, scenario generation agents
9. **Agent Governance Platforms** — Registry, audit, compliance for agent fleets
10. **Agent-to-Agent Economics** — Agents paying each other for services (micro-transactions)

**Recommendations for Experimentation:**
1. Build a "harness-first" agent — invest 80% in context engineering, guardrails, memory
2. Try the Manus pattern: filesystem as memory, sub-agents for isolation, compact dual-form results
3. A/B test persona depth — compare "simple persona" vs "detailed structured persona" for your domain
4. Test tiered routing — what quality drop do you get from small model routing + medium model execution?
5. Experiment with confidence calibration — verbalized confidence vs HTC-style trajectory calibration
6. Try Reflection before output — even for "simple" tasks, does reflection improve quality?
7. Build a minimal evaluation pipeline before your first production agent — eval-driven development
